title: ficus ceriflua
intro: Ficus ceriflua, also known as the wax-leaf fig or banyan fig, is a species of fig tree native to Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Ficus ceriflua include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ficus ceriflua include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. Ficus ceriflua can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
